question: A company tracks how many copies of a board game are sold each year. According to the chart, which statement about the most recent year is most appropriate?

options.A: The most recent year proves that annual copies sold will stay lower from now on.
options.B: The most recent year clearly has the lowest annual sales of the entire period.
options.C: The most recent year shows a sharp drop in sales compared with previous full years.
options.D: The chart only shows sales up to part of the most recent year, so it is not possible to judge the full-year trend yet.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 2 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (330 copies) directly to full-year values like 1,832 copies creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
